channel.title: Awesome Search KG
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Awesome Search KG

item.title: Doug Turnbull
item.description: Doug Turnbull Co-founder of OpenSource Connections (OSC); co-author of Relevant Search (O’Reilly).

item.title: All about Information Retrieval & Search
item.description: Awesome Search — Information Retrieval & Search Knowledge Graph Hello, I am Andrew.

item.title: Elastic
item.description: Elastic What They Build Elastic makes Elasticsearch — the dominant open-source distributed search engine built on Apache Lucene.

item.title: Weaviate
item.description: Weaviate Open-source vector database company providing semantic and hybrid search infrastructure. Competes with Pinecone, Qdrant, Milvus.

item.title: Block-Max WAND
item.description: Block-Max WAND Definition Block-Max WAND (BMW) is an enhancement of the WAND (Weak AND) algorithm that divides posting lists into fixed-size blocks, each with its own local maximum impact score.

item.title: WAND
item.description: WAND (Weighted AND) Definition WAND (Weak AND / Weighted AND) is a top-k retrieval algorithm for sparse vector inner-product scoring that avoids scoring every document in an inverted index.

item.title: Adrien Grand
item.description: Adrien Grand Principal engineer at Elastic, core Apache Lucene committer.

item.title: André Mourão
item.description: André Mourão Search and IR engineer at Weaviate.

item.title: Joon-Pil (JP) Hwang
item.description: Joon-Pil (JP) Hwang Search engineer at Weaviate. Co-implemented Weaviate’s Block-Max WAND keyword search optimization (v1.29 technical preview).

item.title: BlockMax WAND: How Weaviate Achieved 10x Faster Keyword Search
item.description: BlockMax WAND: How Weaviate Achieved 10x Faster Keyword Search Authors: André Mourão, Joon-Pil (JP) Hwang (Weaviate) Published: 2025-02-26 Weaviate implemented Block-Max WAND as a technical preview in v1.29, achieving p50 query speedups of 5–10x and index size reductions of 50–90% over their existin...
